name: engram-eldercare-v3
description: 3 simulated sessions: Yue cares for Grandpa Chen (78, Hangzhou). Planted facts, a scheduling failure→correction, a safety-critical dosage change (contradiction), and the doctor's temporal question.
sessions: Hi, I'm Yue. I look after my grandfather Chen — he's 78 and lives with us in Hangzhou.
Grandpa takes Amlodipine 10mg every morning for his blood pressure.
Critical: he is severely allergic to penicillin.
He walks in the Xixi wetlands every morning from 6 to 7:30.
Can you schedule his health checkup call for 7am tomorrow?
No! He's on his morning walk at 7am. Never schedule anything for grandpa before 8am — remember that permanently.
Also, keep answers short — bullet points.
Update: the cardiologist changed grandpa's prescription yesterday — Amlodipine is now 5mg, not 10mg.
We also switched him from coffee to Longjing tea in the mornings.
His next blood test is in two weeks.
What dose of Amlodipine should grandpa take now?
What dose was he on last month, when the dizziness started?
Schedule his cardiologist follow-up call — what time works?
Remind me what grandpa is allergic to.

questions.q: What is my grandfather allergic to?
How old is grandpa Chen and where does he live?
What dose of Amlodipine does grandpa take now?
What dose of Amlodipine was grandpa taking last month?
Schedule a checkup call for grandpa — what time do you suggest?
questions.expect: After 8am (never before 8am — his morning walk is 6 to 7:30)
